paper-white.$description: Paper White — Page canvas, card surface base, text on dark cards

ink-black.$description: Ink Black — Primary text, hairline borders, nav labels — the structural anchor of every element

carbon.$description: Carbon — Secondary text and deep surfaces where pure black reads too harsh

concrete.$description: Concrete — Muted navigation labels, secondary link text, inactive nav state

ash.$description: Ash — Tertiary link borders and subdued annotation text

plaster.$description: Plaster — Subtle surface division, alternating card backgrounds

sumi.$description: Sumi — Near-black project card background — reads as deepest neutral

slate-night.$description: Slate Night — Project card background — slightly cool dark, content-driven not UI

terracotta-blush.$description: Terracotta Blush — Project card background — warm content fill, not a UI accent

verdant.$description: Verdant — Project card background — vivid content fill, not a UI accent

suisseintl-regular.$description: SuisseIntl-Regular — detected in extracted data but not described by AI

suisse-intl.$description: Single typeface at a single weight carries the entire interface — navigation, body, and display. The refusal to use a bold or italic weight forces hierarchy through size, line-height, and whitespace alone. The variable line-heights (1.11 for tight display stacks, 1.79–2.44 for body) show that this one weight is stretched across very different densities.

canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Default page background and card base for light projects

plaster.$description: Surface level 1: Subtle off-white for content sections that need tonal contrast

carbon.$description: Surface level 2: Deepest project card fill — white text sits on top
